function_code:
CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.accept_invitation_promote_member()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
DECLARE
  v_was_following BOOLEAN;
  v_old_role TEXT;
  v_exists BOOLEAN;
BEGIN
  -- Only execute logic when status transitions to 'accepted'
  IF TG_OP = 'UPDATE' AND NEW.status = 'accepted' AND COALESCE(OLD.status, '') <> NEW.status THEN
    
    -- 1. Verify if a relationship record already exists in channel_members
    SELECT EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.channel_members 
      WHERE channel_id = NEW.channel_id AND user_id = NEW.user_id
    ) INTO v_exists;

    IF v_exists THEN
      -- Fetch their current state configuration before making changes
      SELECT is_following, role INTO v_was_following, v_old_role
      FROM public.channel_members
      WHERE channel_id = NEW.channel_id AND user_id = NEW.user_id;

      -- Safety fallback: If they are already a member, exit early to prevent double-counting
      IF v_old_role = 'member' THEN
        RETURN NEW;
      END IF;

      -- Promote their role configuration and force engagement activation
      UPDATE public.channel_members
      SET role = 'member', is_following = TRUE
      WHERE channel_id = NEW.channel_id AND user_id = NEW.user_id;

      -- Update channel metrics safely using COALESCE
      UPDATE public.channels
      SET 
        members_count = COALESCE(members_count, 0) + 1,
        -- Only increment followers_count if they weren't already tracked as an active follower
        followers_count = COALESCE(followers_count, 0) + (CASE WHEN COALESCE(v_was_following, FALSE) = TRUE THEN 0 ELSE 1 END)
      WHERE id = NEW.channel_id;

    ELSE
      -- 2. If no record exists at all (e.g., accepting a direct system invitation), create it fresh
      INSERT INTO public.channel_members (channel_id, user_id, role, is_following)
      VALUES (NEW.channel_id, NEW.user_id, 'member', TRUE)
      ON CONFLICT (channel_id, user_id) DO UPDATE
        SET role = 'member', is_following = TRUE;

      -- Since they are a brand new addition, increment both counters cleanly
      UPDATE public.channels
      SET 
        members_count = COALESCE(members_count, 0) + 1,
        followers_count = COALESCE(followers_count, 0) + 1
      WHERE id = NEW.channel_id;
    END IF;

  END IF;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.bulk_ingest_youtube_channels(p_payloads jsonb)
 RETURNS void
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  -- 1. Create a lightweight temporary table to stage the batch data
  CREATE TEMP TABLE temp_channel_ingest (
    id uuid,
    username text,
    display_name text,
    avatar_url text,
    description text
  ) ON COMMIT DROP;

  -- 2. Fast unpack the JSON array directly into the temp table
  INSERT INTO temp_channel_ingest (id, username, display_name, avatar_url, description)
  SELECT 
    (value->>'id')::uuid,
    value->>'username',
    value->>'display_name',
    value->>'avatar_url',
    value->>'description'
  FROM jsonb_array_elements(p_payloads);

  -- 3. Bulk merge into Profiles using a unified scan
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, username, display_name, profile_image_url, bio, is_verified)
  SELECT id, username, display_name, avatar_url, description, true
  FROM temp_channel_ingest
  ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE 
  SET 
    display_name = EXCLUDED.display_name, 
    profile_image_url = EXCLUDED.profile_image_url
  WHERE public.profiles.profile_image_url IS DISTINCT FROM EXCLUDED.profile_image_url; 
  -- Note: The WHERE clause prevents unnecessary writes if data hasn't changed!

  -- 4. Bulk merge into Channels
  INSERT INTO public.channels (id, creator_id, name, description, avatar_url, is_discoverable, join_method)
  SELECT id, id, display_name, description, avatar_url, true, 'open'
  FROM temp_channel_ingest
  ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE 
  SET 
    name = EXCLUDED.name, 
    avatar_url = EXCLUDED.avatar_url
  WHERE public.channels.avatar_url IS DISTINCT FROM EXCLUDED.avatar_url;

END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.decrement_profile_status_views_on_delete()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  UPDATE public.profiles
  SET active_status_views_count = GREATEST(active_status_views_count - OLD.views_count, 0)
  WHERE id = OLD.author_id;
  RETURN OLD;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.delete_invitations_on_restriction()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
begin
  if NEW.allow_invitations_by = 'none' then
    delete from public.channel_posts 
    where channel_id = NEW.id 
    and post_type = 'invitation';
  end if;
  return NEW;
end;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_channel_moments(p_user_id uuid, p_channel_id uuid DEFAULT NULL::uuid, p_page_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_page_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(channel_id uuid, channel_name text, channel_avatar_url text, latest_moment_time timestamp with time zone, moments jsonb)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
BEGIN
  IF p_channel_id IS NULL THEN
    -- Global Feed: All channels the user follows that have active moments
    RETURN QUERY
    WITH active_moments AS (
      SELECT 
        cm.channel_id,
        jsonb_agg(
          jsonb_build_object(
            'id', cm.id,
            'media_url', cm.media_url,
            'thumbnail_url', cm.thumbnail_url,
            'caption', cm.caption,
            'media_type', cm.media_type,
            'created_at', cm.created_at,
            'author_id', cm.author_id
          ) ORDER BY cm.created_at ASC
        ) as moments_array,
        MAX(cm.created_at) as max_created_at
      FROM channel_moments cm
      JOIN channel_members mem ON mem.channel_id = cm.channel_id
      WHERE mem.user_id = p_user_id
        AND (cm.expires_at > NOW() OR (cm.expires_at IS NULL AND cm.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'))
      GROUP BY cm.channel_id
    )
    SELECT 
      c.id, 
      c.name, 
      COALESCE(cb.cover_image_url, c.avatar_url) as channel_avatar_url,
      am.max_created_at as latest_moment_time,
      am.moments_array as moments
    FROM channels c
    JOIN active_moments am ON am.channel_id = c.id
    LEFT JOIN channel_branding cb ON cb.channel_id = c.id
    ORDER BY am.max_created_at DESC
    LIMIT p_page_limit OFFSET p_page_offset;

  ELSE
    -- Specific Channel Feed
    RETURN QUERY
    WITH active_moments AS (
      SELECT 
        cm.channel_id,
        jsonb_agg(
          jsonb_build_object(
            'id', cm.id,
            'media_url', cm.media_url,
            'thumbnail_url', cm.thumbnail_url,
            'caption', cm.caption,
            'media_type', cm.media_type,
            'created_at', cm.created_at,
            'author_id', cm.author_id
          ) ORDER BY cm.created_at ASC
        ) as moments_array,
        MAX(cm.created_at) as max_created_at
      FROM channel_moments cm
      WHERE cm.channel_id = p_channel_id
        AND (cm.expires_at > NOW() OR (cm.expires_at IS NULL AND cm.created_at > NOW() - INTERVAL '24 hours'))
      GROUP BY cm.channel_id
    )
    SELECT 
      c.id, 
      c.name, 
      COALESCE(cb.cover_image_url, c.avatar_url) as channel_avatar_url,
      am.max_created_at as latest_moment_time,
      am.moments_array as moments
    FROM channels c
    JOIN active_moments am ON am.channel_id = c.id
    LEFT JOIN channel_branding cb ON cb.channel_id = c.id
    LIMIT 1;
  END IF;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_explore_channels(p_user_id uuid, p_page_offset integer DEFAULT 0, p_page_limit integer DEFAULT 10)
 RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, creator_id uuid, name text, description text, avatar_url text, members_count integer, is_discoverable boolean, join_method text, creator_name text, creator_avatar_url text)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  WITH RECURSIVE
  -- 1. Get the channels the user is currently in (Depth 0)
  user_channels AS (
    SELECT cm.channel_id
    FROM channel_members cm
    WHERE cm.user_id = p_user_id
  ),
  -- 2. Traverse the graph: User -> Channels -> Co-Members -> Their Channels
  channel_tree AS (
    -- Base case: Channels the user is directly in
    SELECT 
      uc.channel_id,
      0 as depth
    FROM user_channels uc

    UNION
    
    -- Recursive step: Find users in the current channels, then find the channels those users are in.
    -- We limit depth to 2 to prevent massive query times.
    SELECT 
      cm2.channel_id,
      ct.depth + 1
    FROM channel_tree ct
    JOIN channel_members cm1 ON cm1.channel_id = ct.channel_id
    JOIN channel_members cm2 ON cm2.user_id = cm1.user_id
    WHERE ct.depth < 2
  ),
  -- 3. Extract candidate channels from the tree, assigning minimum depth
  candidate_tree_channels AS (
    SELECT 
      ct.channel_id,
      MIN(ct.depth) as min_depth
    FROM channel_tree ct
    WHERE ct.depth > 0 -- depth 0 is the user's own channels, which we exclude
    GROUP BY ct.channel_id
  ),
  -- 4. Combine tree channels with ALL other discoverable channels in the DB
  -- Tree channels get their computed depth (1 or 2). Other channels get depth 99.
  all_candidates AS (
    SELECT 
      c.id AS channel_id,
      COALESCE(ctc.min_depth, 99) AS depth
    FROM channels c
    LEFT JOIN candidate_tree_channels ctc ON ctc.channel_id = c.id
    WHERE c.creator_id != p_user_id -- Exclude owned
      AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT uc.channel_id FROM user_channels uc) -- Exclude joined
      AND c.is_discoverable = true
  )
  -- 5. Select the actual channel data, ordering by depth (closest first), then size, then newest
  SELECT 
    c.id,
    c.creator_id,
    c.name,
    c.description,
    c.avatar_url,
    c.members_count,
    c.is_discoverable,
    c.join_method,
    u.display_name AS creator_name,
    u.profile_image_url AS creator_avatar_url
  FROM all_candidates ac
  JOIN channels c ON c.id = ac.channel_id
  LEFT JOIN profiles u ON u.id = c.creator_id
  ORDER BY ac.depth ASC, c.members_count DESC, c.created_at DESC, c.id ASC
  OFFSET p_page_offset
  LIMIT p_page_limit;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_managed_channels(user_id_param uuid, limit_param integer DEFAULT 10, offset_param integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, creator_id uuid, name text, description text, avatar_url text, age_restriction text, members_count integer, followers_count integer, created_at timestamp with time zone, user_role text)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT DISTINCT ON (c.id)
    c.id,
    c.creator_id,
    c.name,
    c.description,
    c.avatar_url,
    c.age_restriction,
    c.members_count,
    c.followers_count,
    c.created_at,
    CASE WHEN c.creator_id = user_id_param THEN 'creator' ELSE COALESCE(cm.role, 'follower') END AS user_role
  FROM public.channels c
  LEFT JOIN public.channel_members cm
    ON c.id = cm.channel_id AND cm.user_id = user_id_param
  WHERE
    -- They created the channel
    c.creator_id = user_id_param
    -- Or they have an explicit role we care about (whitelist)
    OR (cm.user_id = user_id_param AND cm.role IN ('admin', 'member', 'follower'))
  ORDER BY c.id, c.created_at DESC
  LIMIT limit_param
  OFFSET offset_param;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_my_associated_channels()
 RETURNS SETOF channels
 LANGUAGE sql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
  SELECT c.*
  FROM public.channels c
  WHERE 
    c.creator_id = auth.uid()
    OR 
    EXISTS (
      SELECT 1 FROM public.channel_members m 
      WHERE m.channel_id = c.id AND m.user_id = auth.uid()
    );
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_profile_suggested_channels(p_profile_user_id uuid, p_current_user_id uuid, p_page_offset integer DEFAULT 0, p_page_limit integer DEFAULT 10)
 RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, creator_id uuid, name text, description text, avatar_url text, members_count integer, is_discoverable boolean, join_method text, creator_name text, creator_avatar_url text)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  WITH RECURSIVE
  -- 1. Base case: The profile user's channels
  user_channels AS (
    SELECT cm.channel_id FROM channel_members cm WHERE cm.user_id = p_profile_user_id
  ),
  -- 2. Build the social tree up to depth 2
  channel_tree AS (
    SELECT 
      uc.channel_id,
      0 AS depth
    FROM user_channels uc
    
    UNION
    
    SELECT 
      cm2.channel_id,
      ct.depth + 1
    FROM channel_tree ct
    JOIN channel_members cm1 ON cm1.channel_id = ct.channel_id
    JOIN channel_members cm2 ON cm2.user_id = cm1.user_id
    WHERE ct.depth < 2
  ),
  -- 3. Extract candidate channels from the tree
  candidate_tree_channels AS (
    SELECT 
      ct.channel_id,
      MIN(ct.depth) as min_depth
    FROM channel_tree ct
    GROUP BY ct.channel_id
  ),
  -- 4. Combine tree channels with ALL other discoverable channels
  all_candidates AS (
    SELECT 
      c.id AS channel_id,
      COALESCE(ctc.min_depth, 99) AS depth
    FROM channels c
    LEFT JOIN candidate_tree_channels ctc ON ctc.channel_id = c.id
    WHERE c.creator_id != p_current_user_id -- Exclude channels created by the CURRENT user
      AND c.id NOT IN (SELECT uc_current.channel_id FROM channel_members uc_current WHERE uc_current.user_id = p_current_user_id) -- Exclude channels the CURRENT user has already joined
      AND c.is_discoverable = true
  )
  -- 5. Select the actual channel data, ordering by depth (closest to the profile user first), then size, then newest
  SELECT 
    c.id,
    c.creator_id,
    c.name,
    c.description,
    c.avatar_url,
    c.members_count,
    c.is_discoverable,
    c.join_method,
    u.display_name AS creator_name,
    u.profile_image_url AS creator_avatar_url
  FROM all_candidates ac
  JOIN channels c ON c.id = ac.channel_id
  LEFT JOIN profiles u ON u.id = c.creator_id
  ORDER BY ac.depth ASC, c.members_count DESC, c.created_at DESC, c.id ASC
  OFFSET p_page_offset
  LIMIT p_page_limit;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_social_discovery_channels(p_user_id uuid, p_limit integer DEFAULT 20, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(id text, name text, description text, avatar_url text, join_method text, members_count integer, followers_count integer, is_following boolean, is_member boolean)
 LANGUAGE sql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$

WITH

-- Channels the user is already in
my_channels AS (
  SELECT channel_id
  FROM   public.channel_members
  WHERE  user_id      = p_user_id
    AND  is_following = true
),

-- Users who share at least one channel with me
my_peers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT cm.user_id
  FROM   public.channel_members cm
  WHERE  cm.channel_id IN (SELECT channel_id FROM my_channels)
    AND  cm.user_id   != p_user_id
),

-- Open/discoverable channels those peers belong to (that I'm NOT already in)
discovery_channels AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT cm.channel_id
  FROM   public.channel_members cm
  JOIN   public.channels        c ON c.id = cm.channel_id
  WHERE  cm.user_id    IN (SELECT user_id FROM my_peers)
    AND  cm.channel_id NOT IN (SELECT channel_id FROM my_channels)
    AND  c.is_discoverable = true
    AND  c.join_method IN ('open', 'anyone')
    -- Age / country restriction hooks:
    -- AND c.age_restriction = 'All Ages'
)

SELECT
  c.id::TEXT,
  c.name,
  c.description,
  -- channels has no avatar_url; use channel_branding cover if available
  cb.cover_image_url                       AS avatar_url,
  c.join_method,
  COALESCE(c.members_count,   0)           AS members_count,
  COALESCE(c.followers_count, 0)           AS followers_count,
  -- is_following: the caller already follows this channel
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.channel_members cm2
    WHERE  cm2.channel_id  = c.id
      AND  cm2.user_id     = p_user_id
      AND  cm2.is_following = true
  )                                        AS is_following,
  -- is_member: the caller is a full member
  EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.channel_members cm3
    WHERE  cm3.channel_id = c.id
      AND  cm3.user_id    = p_user_id
      AND  cm3.role IN ('member', 'admin', 'owner')
  )                                        AS is_member
FROM   public.channels       c
LEFT   JOIN public.channel_branding cb ON cb.channel_id = c.id
WHERE  c.id IN (SELECT channel_id FROM discovery_channels)
ORDER  BY c.members_count DESC
LIMIT  p_limit
OFFSET p_offset;

$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_social_discovery_feed(p_user_id uuid, p_limit integer DEFAULT 20, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(id text, author_id text, author_username text, author_avatar_url text, channel_id text, channel_name text, channel_avatar_url text, caption text, video_url text, image_urls text, is_video boolean, likes integer, comments integer, created_at timestamp with time zone, widget_type text)
 LANGUAGE sql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$

WITH

my_channels AS (
  SELECT channel_id
  FROM   public.channel_members
  WHERE  user_id      = p_user_id
    AND  is_following = true
),

my_peers AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT cm.user_id
  FROM   public.channel_members cm
  WHERE  cm.channel_id IN (SELECT channel_id FROM my_channels)
    AND  cm.user_id   != p_user_id
),

discovery_channels AS (
  SELECT DISTINCT cm.channel_id
  FROM   public.channel_members cm
  JOIN   public.channels        c ON c.id::text = cm.channel_id::text
  WHERE  cm.user_id IN (SELECT user_id FROM my_peers)
    AND  c.is_discoverable = true
    AND  c.join_method IN ('open', 'anyone')
),

all_visible_channels AS (
  SELECT channel_id FROM my_channels
  UNION
  SELECT channel_id FROM discovery_channels
),

channel_post_rows AS (
  SELECT
    ('cp_' || cp.id::TEXT)                           AS id,
    cp.author_id::TEXT                               AS author_id,
    COALESCE(cp.author_username, pr.display_name, 'member') AS author_username,
    COALESCE(cp.author_profile_image_url, pr.profile_image_url)       AS author_avatar_url,
    cp.channel_id::TEXT                              AS channel_id,
    COALESCE(NULLIF(c.name, ''), 'Channel')         AS channel_name,
    COALESCE(cb.leader_avatar_url, c.avatar_url, pr.profile_image_url, '') AS channel_avatar_url,
    COALESCE(cp.caption, '')                         AS caption,
    cp.video_url,
    COALESCE(cp.image_urls::TEXT, '[]')              AS image_urls,
    COALESCE(cp.is_video, false)                     AS is_video,
    COALESCE(cp.likes_count, 0)                      AS likes,
    COALESCE(cp.comments_count, 0)                   AS comments,
    cp.created_at,
    'channel_post'::TEXT                             AS widget_type
  FROM   public.channel_posts    cp
  JOIN   public.channels         c  ON c.id::text = cp.channel_id::text
  LEFT   JOIN public.channel_branding cb ON cb.channel_id = c.id
  LEFT   JOIN public.profiles    pr ON pr.id = cp.author_id
  WHERE  true
),

regular_post_rows AS (
  SELECT
    ('rp_' || po.id::TEXT)                           AS id,
    po.author_id::TEXT                               AS author_id,
    COALESCE(pr.display_name, 'member') AS author_username,
    COALESCE(pr.profile_image_url, '')               AS author_avatar_url,
    NULL::TEXT                                       AS channel_id, 
    'Personal Post'::TEXT                            AS channel_name, 
    COALESCE(pr.profile_image_url, '')               AS channel_avatar_url, 
    COALESCE(po.caption, '')                         AS caption,
    po.video_url,
    COALESCE(po.image_urls::TEXT, '[]')              AS image_urls,
    COALESCE(po.is_video, false)                     AS is_video,
    COALESCE(po.likes_count, 0)                      AS likes,
    COALESCE(po.comments_count, 0)                   AS comments,
    po.created_at,
    'regular_post'::TEXT                             AS widget_type
  FROM   public.posts      po
  LEFT   JOIN public.profiles  pr  ON pr.id = po.author_id
  WHERE  true
)

SELECT * FROM channel_post_rows
UNION ALL
SELECT * FROM regular_post_rows
ORDER  BY created_at DESC
LIMIT  p_limit
OFFSET p_offset;

$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_unique_status_viewers(p_author_id uuid, p_limit integer, p_offset integer)
 RETURNS TABLE(viewer_id uuid, display_name text, profile_image_url text, last_viewed_at timestamp with time zone, viewed_statuses jsonb)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  RETURN QUERY
  SELECT 
    v.viewer_id,
    p.display_name,
    p.profile_image_url,
    MAX(v.created_at) as last_viewed_at,
    jsonb_agg(
      jsonb_build_object(
        'id', s.id,
        'image_urls', s.image_urls,
        'video_url', s.video_url,
        'thumbnail_url', s.thumbnail_url,
        'viewed_at', v.created_at
      ) ORDER BY v.created_at DESC
    ) as viewed_statuses
  FROM status_views v
  JOIN statuses s ON v.status_id = s.id
  JOIN profiles p ON v.viewer_id = p.id
  WHERE s.author_id = p_author_id
    AND s.expires_at > now()
  GROUP BY v.viewer_id, p.display_name, p.profile_image_url
  ORDER BY last_viewed_at DESC
  LIMIT p_limit
  OFFSET p_offset;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_unique_status_viewers_count(p_author_id uuid)
 RETURNS integer
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
DECLARE
  total_views int;
BEGIN
  SELECT COUNT(*)
  INTO total_views
  FROM status_views v
  JOIN statuses s ON v.status_id = s.id
  WHERE s.author_id = p_author_id
    AND s.expires_at > now();
    
  RETURN COALESCE(total_views, 0);
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_channels(p_user_id uuid, p_target_user_id uuid, p_filter_type text, p_page_offset integer DEFAULT 0, p_page_limit integer DEFAULT 10)
 RETURNS TABLE(id uuid, creator_id uuid, name text, description text, avatar_url text, members_count integer, is_discoverable boolean, created_at timestamp with time zone, creator_name text, creator_avatar_url text, channel_type text, unread_count integer, moments_count integer, messages_count integer, tags_count integer, likes_count integer, followers_count integer, has_active_members boolean)
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
AS $function$
BEGIN
  IF p_filter_type = 'owned' THEN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT 
      c.id, c.creator_id, c.name, c.description, c.avatar_url, c.members_count, c.is_discoverable, c.created_at,
      p.display_name as creator_name, p.profile_image_url as creator_avatar_url,
      'owned'::TEXT as channel_type,
      COALESCE(cm.unread_count, 0) as unread_count,
      c.moments_count, c.messages_count, c.tags_count, c.likes_count, c.followers_count, c.has_active_members
    FROM channels c
    LEFT JOIN profiles p ON p.id = c.creator_id
    LEFT JOIN channel_members cm ON cm.channel_id = c.id AND cm.user_id = p_user_id
    WHERE c.creator_id = p_user_id
    ORDER BY c.created_at DESC
    LIMIT p_page_limit OFFSET p_page_offset;

  ELSIF p_filter_type = 'joined' THEN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT 
      c.id, c.creator_id, c.name, c.description, c.avatar_url, c.members_count, c.is_discoverable, c.created_at,
      p.display_name as creator_name, p.profile_image_url as creator_avatar_url,
      'joined'::TEXT as channel_type,
      COALESCE(cm.unread_count, 0) as unread_count,
      c.moments_count, c.messages_count, c.tags_count, c.likes_count, c.followers_count, c.has_active_members
    FROM channels c
    JOIN channel_members cm ON cm.channel_id = c.id
    LEFT JOIN profiles p ON p.id = c.creator_id
    WHERE cm.user_id = p_user_id
    ORDER BY cm.joined_at DESC
    LIMIT p_page_limit OFFSET p_page_offset;

  ELSIF p_filter_type = 'similar' THEN
    RETURN QUERY
    SELECT 
      c.id, c.creator_id, c.name, c.description, c.avatar_url, c.members_count, c.is_discoverable, c.created_at,
      p.display_name as creator_name, p.profile_image_url as creator_avatar_url,
      'similar'::TEXT as channel_type,
      0 as unread_count,
      c.moments_count, c.messages_count, c.tags_count, c.likes_count, c.followers_count, c.has_active_members
    FROM channels c
    JOIN channel_members cm1 ON cm1.channel_id = c.id
    JOIN channel_members cm2 ON cm2.channel_id = c.id
    LEFT JOIN profiles p ON p.id = c.creator_id
    WHERE cm1.user_id = p_user_id AND cm2.user_id = p_target_user_id
    ORDER BY c.members_count DESC
    LIMIT p_page_limit OFFSET p_page_offset;
  END IF;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.get_user_profile_media(p_user_id uuid, p_media_type text, p_limit integer DEFAULT 10, p_offset integer DEFAULT 0)
 RETURNS TABLE(id text, author_id text, caption text, video_url text, audio_url text, image_urls jsonb, thumbnail_urls jsonb, is_video boolean, is_audio boolean, likes_count integer, comments_count integer, created_at timestamp with time zone, metadata jsonb)
 LANGUAGE sql
 STABLE SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
  SELECT 
    id::TEXT,
    author_id::TEXT,
    COALESCE(caption, ''),
    video_url,
    audio_url,
    COALESCE(image_urls::JSONB, '[]'::JSONB),
    COALESCE(thumbnail_urls::JSONB, '[]'::JSONB),
    COALESCE(is_video, false),
    COALESCE(is_audio, false),
    COALESCE(likes_count, 0),
    COALESCE(comments_count, 0),
    created_at,
    COALESCE(metadata, '{}'::JSONB)
  FROM public.posts
  WHERE author_id = p_user_id
  AND (
    (p_media_type = 'video' AND is_video = true) OR
    (p_media_type = 'audio' AND is_audio = true) OR
    (p_media_type = 'photo' AND is_video = false AND is_audio = false AND (jsonb_array_length(COALESCE(image_urls::JSONB, '[]'::JSONB)) > 0))
  )
  ORDER BY created_at DESC
  LIMIT p_limit OFFSET p_offset;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_channel_creator()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.channel_members (channel_id, user_id, role)
  VALUES (NEW.id, NEW.creator_id, 'admin');
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.handle_new_user()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.profiles (id, username, display_name, profile_image_url)
  VALUES (
    NEW.id,
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'username', NEW.email), -- Fallback to email if no username
    COALESCE(NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'full_name', NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'name', NEW.email),
    NEW.raw_user_meta_data->>'avatar_url'
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.increment_manifesto_comment_count()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
BEGIN
  IF NEW.manifesto_id IS NOT NULL THEN
    UPDATE public.manifestos
    SET comments = comments + 1
    WHERE id = NEW.manifesto_id;
  END IF;
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.increment_post_comment_count(target_post_id uuid)
 RETURNS void
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  -- 1. Increment counter on the new channel_posts table
  UPDATE public.channel_posts
  SET comments = COALESCE(comments, 0) + 1
  WHERE id = target_post_id;

  -- 2. Increment counter on the legacy posts table (just in case)
  UPDATE public.posts
  SET comments = COALESCE(comments, 0) + 1
  WHERE id = target_post_id;

  -- 3. Trigger real-time sync (Safe version: won't crash if logged out)
  IF auth.uid() IS NOT NULL THEN
    INSERT INTO public.channel_post_comment_counts (post_id, user_id, created_at)
    VALUES (target_post_id, auth.uid(), now())
    ON CONFLICT (post_id, user_id) 
    DO UPDATE SET created_at = now();
  END IF;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.increment_unread_count()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  UPDATE channel_members
  SET unread_count = unread_count + 1
  WHERE 
    channel_id = NEW.channel_id
    AND user_id != NEW.sender_id;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.increment_unread_moments_count()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  UPDATE channel_members
  SET unread_moments_count = unread_moments_count + 1
  WHERE 
    channel_id = NEW.channel_id
    AND user_id != NEW.author_id;  -- author_id confirmed from channel_moments schema

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_channel_creator(check_channel_id uuid)
 RETURNS boolean
 LANGUAGE sql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.channels
    WHERE id = check_channel_id AND creator_id = auth.uid()
  );
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.is_channel_member(check_channel_id uuid)
 RETURNS boolean
 LANGUAGE sql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
  SELECT EXISTS (
    SELECT 1 FROM public.channel_members
    WHERE channel_id = check_channel_id AND user_id = auth.uid()
  );
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.log_to_app_events()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
 SET search_path TO 'public'
AS $function$
BEGIN
  INSERT INTO public.app_events (table_name, action_type, record_id, data)
  VALUES (
    TG_TABLE_NAME, 
    TG_OP, 
    (row_to_json(COALESCE(NEW, OLD))->>'id')::uuid, 
    row_to_json(COALESCE(NEW, OLD))::jsonb
  );
  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.send_first_message(p_thread_id uuid, p_participant_id uuid, p_message_id text, p_body text)
 RETURNS void
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  -- 1. Create the inbox entry for the sender
  INSERT INTO public.inbox (id, user_id, participant_id, last_message)
  VALUES (p_thread_id, auth.uid(), p_participant_id, p_body);

  -- 2. Insert the message
  INSERT INTO public.inbox_messages (id, thread_id, sender_id, body)
  VALUES (p_message_id, p_thread_id, auth.uid(), p_body);
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.send_inbox_message(p_thread_id uuid, p_sender_id uuid, p_recipient_id uuid, p_body text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_media_url text DEFAULT NULL::text, p_message_type text DEFAULT 'text'::text, p_message_id text DEFAULT (gen_random_uuid())::text)
 RETURNS void
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
DECLARE
  v_now timestamptz := now();
  v_sender_exists boolean;
  v_recipient_exists boolean;
BEGIN
  -- Guard: caller must be the sender
  IF p_sender_id != auth.uid() THEN
    RAISE EXCEPTION 'Unauthorized: sender_id does not match auth.uid()';
  END IF;

  -- Insert the message first (id is text)
  INSERT INTO public.inbox_messages (id, thread_id, sender_id, body, media_url, message_type, created_at)
  VALUES (p_message_id, p_thread_id, p_sender_id, p_body, p_media_url, p_message_type, v_now)
  ON CONFLICT (id) DO NOTHING;

  -- Verify both profiles exist to prevent foreign key crashes on the inbox table
  SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_sender_id) INTO v_sender_exists;
  SELECT EXISTS(SELECT 1 FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_recipient_id) INTO v_recipient_exists;

  -- Only write to inbox if the profiles exist to satisfy foreign keys
  IF v_sender_exists THEN
    INSERT INTO public.inbox (
      id, user_id, participant_id, participant_name, participant_avatar,
      last_message, last_message_at, unread_count, chat_type
    )
    VALUES (
      p_thread_id,
      p_sender_id,
      p_recipient_id,
      (SELECT display_name FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_recipient_id LIMIT 1),
      (SELECT profile_image_url FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_recipient_id LIMIT 1),
      p_body,
      v_now,
      0,
      'private'
    )
    ON CONFLICT (id, user_id) DO UPDATE
      SET last_message    = EXCLUDED.last_message,
          last_message_at = EXCLUDED.last_message_at,
          participant_name = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.participant_name, public.inbox.participant_name),
          participant_avatar = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.participant_avatar, public.inbox.participant_avatar);
  END IF;

  IF v_recipient_exists THEN
    INSERT INTO public.inbox (
      id, user_id, participant_id, participant_name, participant_avatar,
      last_message, last_message_at, unread_count, chat_type
    )
    VALUES (
      p_thread_id,
      p_recipient_id,
      p_sender_id,
      (SELECT display_name FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_sender_id LIMIT 1),
      (SELECT profile_image_url FROM public.profiles WHERE id = p_sender_id LIMIT 1),
      p_body,
      v_now,
      1,
      'private'
    )
    ON CONFLICT (id, user_id) DO UPDATE
      SET last_message    = EXCLUDED.last_message,
          last_message_at = EXCLUDED.last_message_at,
          unread_count    = public.inbox.unread_count + 1,
          participant_name = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.participant_name, public.inbox.participant_name),
          participant_avatar = COALESCE(EXCLUDED.participant_avatar, public.inbox.participant_avatar);
  END IF;
END;
$function$

CREATE OR REPLACE FUNCTION public.update_inbox_on_message()
 RETURNS trigger
 LANGUAGE plpgsql
 SECURITY DEFINER
AS $function$
BEGIN
  -- Update the sender's inbox (don't increment unread_count)
  UPDATE public.inbox
  SET 
    last_message = NEW.body,
    last_message_type = NEW.message_type,
    last_message_at = NEW.created_at,
    unread_count = 0
  WHERE id = NEW.thread_id AND user_id = NEW.sender_id;

  -- Update the recipient's inbox (increment unread_count)
  UPDATE public.inbox
  SET 
    last_message = NEW.body,
    last_message_type = NEW.message_type,
    last_message_at = NEW.created_at,
    unread_count = unread_count + 1
  WHERE id = NEW.thread_id AND user_id != NEW.sender_id;

  RETURN NEW;
END;
$function$
